pattern: Counting, for every candidate divisor from largest to smallest, how many elements are multiples of it lets inclusion-exclusion peel off the exact count of pairs whose gcd is precisely that divisor, so a running prefix over divisors answers any rank query with a binary search.
hints: For a fixed value d, counting how many array elements are divisible by d tells you, via a combination count, how many pairs have a gcd that is a multiple of d -- not necessarily exactly d.

Process candidate gcd values from largest down to smallest: subtract off the already-known exact counts for every larger multiple of d, and what remains is the exact number of pairs whose gcd is exactly d.

Once you have, for every value from 1 up to the maximum array element, the exact count of pairs with that gcd, a running prefix sum over those counts turns each query into a binary search for the smallest divisor whose prefix sum exceeds the query's rank.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible array, two equal elements, only one pair
all identical elements, every pair gcd equals the element itself
powers of two, many pairs share intermediate gcds
one value is 1, so every pair touching it has gcd 1
maximum value repeated at the value bound

stress.description: random moderate-size array with random values, queries spanning the full pair rank range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50000) for _ in range(n)]
    total_pairs = n * (n - 1) // 2
    q = min(500, total_pairs)
    queries = [rng.randint(0, total_pairs - 1) for _ in range(q)]
    return [nums, queries]

stress.description: array with heavy duplication (few distinct values) to stress the divisor counting
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 3000))
    pool = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(8)]
    nums = [rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n)]
    total_pairs = n * (n - 1) // 2
    queries = [rng.randint(0, total_pairs - 1) for _ in range(200)]
    return [nums, queries]
